title: Elogio Al Aprendizaje
content:
[Original]

ï¿½Aprende las cosas elementarias!
ï¿½Para aquellos a quienes les ha llegado la hora nunca es demasadio tarde!
Aprende el abecedario. No bastarï¿½,
ï¿½pero aprï¿½ndolo! ï¿½No dejes que te desanimen!
ï¿½Comienza! Debes saber todo.
Tienes que ser dirigente.
ï¿½Aprende, hombre en el asilo!
ï¿½Aprende, hombre en la prisiï¿½n!
ï¿½Aprende, mujer en la cocina!
ï¿½Aprende, tï¿½ que tienes 60 aï¿½os!
Tienes que ser dirigente.
ï¿½Busca la esquela, tï¿½ que no tienes casa!

ï¿½No tengas miedo de preguntar, camarada!
No dejes que te induzcan a nada.
ï¿½Investiga por ti mismo!
Lo que no sepas tï¿½ mismo no lo conoces.
Examina los detalles a fondo;
eres tï¿½ ï¿½l que paga las consequencias.
Pon tu dedo en cada detalle, pregunta: ï¿½Cï¿½mo llegï¿½ esto aqui?
Tienes que ser dirigente.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Bertolt Brecht represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Bertolt Brecht's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
